wines.name: La Marca Prosecco
sources: https://www.wine.com/product/la-marca-prosecco/24828
ABV 11.2% confirmed on Wine.com product page → Alcohol 2
Winemaker notes: 'light and refreshing', 'hint of sweetness' — Extra Dry Prosecco style, ~12g/L RS → Sweetness 2
James Suckling: 'Light body. Frothy and crisp' → Body 2, Acidity 4

wines.name: La Marca Prosecco Rose 2023
sources: https://www.wine.com/product/la-marca-prosecco-rose-2023/1988560
inference: ABV not listed on Wine.com 2024 product page; same producer and Prosecco DOC as La Marca NV (ABV 11.2%) — sibling wine inferred at ~11.0% → Alcohol 2
inference: Prosecco Rosé sparkling, same house style as La Marca NV — crisp, Extra Dry style, light body → Tannin 1, Acidity 4, Sweetness 2, Body 2

wines.name: Laurent-Perrier La Cuvee Brut (375ML half-bottle)
sources: https://www.wine.com/product/laurent-perrier-la-cuvee-brut-375ml-half-bottle/330992
ABV 12% confirmed on Wine.com product page → Alcohol 2
James Suckling 94: 'Medium-bodied and structured with solid intensity' — Champagne Brut style
Robert Parker 90: 'dry and silky textured, vivacious, and perfectly fresh' → Sweetness 1
Wine Spectator 91: 'finely honed acidity' — Champagne baseline → Acidity 5
Wine & Spirits 90: 'spicy, tight and formidable' — confirms Champagne structure

wines.name: Iron Horse Wedding Cuvee 2019
sources: https://www.wine.com/product/iron-horse-wedding-cuvee-2019/1273434
ABV 13.5% confirmed on Wine.com product page → Alcohol 4 (boundary rounds up)
James Suckling 93: 'light-bodied but full-flavored', 'Nicely dry' → Body 2, Sweetness 1
Wine Spectator 93: 'Succulent and seductive' with 'creamy finish' — traditional method California sparkling → Acidity 5

wines.name: Hampton Water Bubbly Rose
sources: https://www.wine.com/product/hampton-water-bubbly-rose/2206635
ABV 13% confirmed on Wine.com product page → Alcohol 3
Winemaker notes: 'fresh, well-balanced, and intense, with fine, persistent bubbles' — sparkling rosé, South of France
Wilfred Wong 91: 'refreshing and delicious' — tank-method sparkling rosé; Grenache/Pinot Noir/Chardonnay blend → Acidity 4 (not Champagne-level), Sweetness 2 (Brut dry style), Body 2

wines.name: Miraval Rose 2024
sources: https://www.wine.com/product/miraval-rose-2024/3036255
ABV 13% confirmed on Wine.com product page → Alcohol 3
James Suckling 92: 'medium-bodied palate is sleek and finely textured, driven by bright acidity' → Body 2, Acidity 4
Robert Parker 90: 'moderately weighted, juicy and round' — confirms Côtes de Provence bone-dry style → Sweetness 1

wines.name: Chateau d'Esclans Whispering Angel Rose 2024
sources: https://www.wine.com/product/chateau-desclans-whispering-angel-rose-2024/2847780
ABV 13% confirmed on Wine.com product page → Alcohol 3
James Suckling 92: 'Medium-bodied with bright acidity. Balanced and driven' → Body 2, Acidity 4
Winemaker notes: 'Smooth and round finish' — Côtes de Provence dry rosé → Sweetness 1, Tannin 1

wines.name: Chateau Montaud Cotes de Provence Rose 2024
sources: https://www.wine.com/product/chateau-montaud-cotes-de-provence-rose-2024/3160572
ABV 13% confirmed on Wine.com product page → Alcohol 3
Winemaker notes: 'dry, crisp and refreshing' → Sweetness 1, Acidity 4
Wine Enthusiast 90: 'stony grapefruit flavors' — classic Provence rosé profile → Body 2, Tannin 1

wines.name: Chateau D'Aqueria Tavel Rose 2024
sources: https://www.wine.com/product/chateau-daqueria-tavel-rose-2024/3063489
ABV 14% confirmed on Wine.com product page → Alcohol 4
Robert Parker 91: 'medium- to full-bodied, round and enveloping wine with a structured mid-palate' → Body 3
Tasting Panel 94: 'wonderfully deep flavors... streak of acidity' → Acidity 4, slight Grenache/Syrah grip → Tannin 2
Wine.com region description: 'always dry' for Tavel appellation → Sweetness 1

wines.name: Hampton Water Rose 2024
sources: https://www.wine.com/product/hampton-water-rose-2024/2929745

inference: ABV not listed for 2024 vintage on Wine.com; prior vintages (2018–2023) consistently scored 90–91 pts from Decanter/WE/WS — Gérard Bertrand Languedoc Grenache/Cinsault house style = ~13% ABV → Alcohol 3
Winemaker notes: 'fresh and lively, with soft notes of strawberry, and citrus; aged in new French Oak barrels' — oak aging softens acidity vs Provence baseline → Acidity 3
inference: Languedoc Grenache/Cinsault dry rosé, bone dry Gérard Bertrand house style → Sweetness 1, Tannin 1, Body 2

wines.name: Schug Sonoma Coast Pinot Noir 2023
sources: https://www.wine.com/product/schug-sonoma-coast-pinot-noir-2023/2283884
ABV 13.8% confirmed on Wine.com product page → Alcohol 4
James Suckling 91: 'The palate is light-bodied with bright acidity' → Body 2, Acidity 4
Winemaker notes: 'Lively acidity on the finish', neutral oak aging preserves fresh red-fruit character → Tannin 3 (Pinot Noir baseline), Sweetness 1

wines.name: Chehalem Estate Grown Chehalem Mountains Pinot Noir 2022
sources: https://www.wine.com/product/chehalem-estate-grown-chehalem-mountains-pinot-noir-2022/2175259
ABV 14% confirmed on Wine.com product page → Alcohol 4 (boundary rounds up)
Winemaker notes: 'medium body and dusty tannin structure' → Body 3, Tannin 3
Wine Spectator 93: 'refined tannins' — Chehalem Mountains cool-climate Willamette Valley → Acidity 4
Jeb Dunnuck 90: 'medium-bodied, with a snappy freshness' — confirms Body 3, Acidity 4

wines.name: Lemelson Thea's Selection Pinot Noir 2022
sources: https://www.wine.com/product/lemelson-theas-selection-pinot-noir-2022/2560816
ABV 13.5% confirmed on Wine.com product page → Alcohol 4 (boundary rounds up)
Vinous 91: 'gently tannic, tapering off perfumed and fresh' → Tannin 3
Wine Spectator 91: 'Snappy and vibrant... sleek tannins' — Willamette Valley cool-climate → Acidity 4
Jeb Dunnuck 90: 'bright and medium-bodied' → Body 3
Winemaker notes: 'rich, silky tannins give in to a long and robust finish' — confirms medium tannin → Tannin 3

wines.name: Fonseca Bin No. 27 Port
sources: https://www.wine.com/product/fonseca-bin-no-27-port/1515
ABV 20% confirmed on Wine.com product page → Alcohol 5
Winemaker notes: 'full-bodied and round, with a soft and velvety texture, balanced by firm tannins' → Body 5, Tannin 3, Sweetness 5 (Ruby Port fortified ~100g/L RS)
Decanter 93: 'firm tannic grip' — Ruby Reserve Port baseline → Tannin 3, Acidity 3
Wine & Spirits 92: 'freshness firmed up by schist tannins' — medium acidity for Port style

wines.name: Graham's Six Grapes Reserve Ruby Port
sources: https://www.wine.com/product/grahams-six-grapes-reserve-ruby-port/5968
ABV 19.5% confirmed on Wine.com product page → Alcohol 5
Winemaker notes: 'very full-bodied, luscious wine' → Body 5
Decanter 95: 'well balanced tannins and freshness' → Tannin 3, Acidity 3
Wilfred Wong 91: 'Deep, rich, and satiny' — Ruby Reserve Port style → Sweetness 5 (fortified, ~100g/L RS)
